bill.constitutionalAuthorityStatementText: [Congressional Record Volume 172, Number 66 (Wednesday, April 15, 2026)][House]From the Congressional Record Online through the Government Publishing Office [www.gpo.gov]By Mr. STEIL:H.R. 8286.Congress has the power to enact this legislation pursuantto the following:Article I, Section 8 of the US Constitution: To regulatecommerce with foreign nations, and among the several states,and with the Indian tribes[Page H2925]

item.text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

bill.title: Protecting Americans’ Retirement Savings From Politics Act

item.title: Protecting Americans’ Retirement Savings From Politics Act
Protecting Americans’ Retirement Savings From Politics Act
To amend the Federal securities laws with respect to the materiality of disclosure requirements, to establish the Public Company Advisory Committee, and for other purposes.

latestAction.text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.
dublinCore.dc:contributor: Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress
dublinCore.dc:description: This file contains bill summaries and statuses for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is "to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.
